step.text: Obtain DSC, DIN for minimum 3 directors
step.name: Step 2

step.text: Draft MOA and AOA (no share transfer restrictions)
step.name: Step 3

mainEntity.name: What is the minimum capital for public company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Minimum authorised capital of ₹5 lakhs (reduced from ₹5 crore in 2015). Paid-up capital: no statutory minimum.

mainEntity.name: Can a public company be unlisted?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Many public companies are unlisted. Listing on BSE/NSE is optional but requires SEBI ICDR compliance.

mainEntity.name: What are the requirements for public limited company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Minimum 3 directors, 7 shareholders, ₹5 lakh authorised capital. Must have company secretary if paid-up capital >₹5 crore. Subject to SEBI regulations if listed. Higher compliance than private companies.

mainEntity.name: What is the compliance difference vs private company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Public company: mandatory CS, public disclosure of financials, no restriction on shareholders (max 200 for private), secretarial audit if capital >₹50 crore, and SEBI compliance if listed.

mainEntity.name: How to convert private company to public?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pass special resolution, alter Articles removing Pvt Ltd restrictions, file MGT-14 with RoC, apply for name change (remove Private), file INC-27 for conversion. Timeline: 1-2 months.
